reasoning: The question asks if NOAA's Climate.gov will be online according to 'Is It Down Right Now?' on July 1, 2025. As of early May 2025, the website is up. Evidence suggests that while the Trump administration has been scrubbing, halting updates, or archiving climate-related data, the entire Climate.gov domain is unlikely to become completely unresponsive by July 2025. Announcements from NOAA indicate that "final updates" have been posted, suggesting the site is being transitioned into an archived/static state rather than being completely deleted or taken offline. Furthermore, technical inertia and federal records rules mean that even if the program is defunded, the domain will likely remain active, serve static content, or set up a redirect (all of which would result in 'Is It Down Right Now' recognizing the site as UP, since it simply checks for server responsiveness/DNS resolution). Given the short 1.5-month time horizon from the knowledge cutoff and the technical definitions of a site being "up," the probability is very high.
